title: Field-season-board splitting wave
summary: Move another chunk of season/route/station composition out of the large board resolver.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: field-season-board.ts carries less responsibility or exposes clearer pure helpers, with tests for route/station states.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Extract a pure resolver for one board family: atlas/archive, outing locator, filed-state summary, or station subtitle.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Provide expected copy strings and content fixtures; do not edit resolver logic directly.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: No geometry work unless the resolver extraction exposes a visual regression in board placement.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Assert Route v2 active/ready/filed/replay state remains identical after the board split.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.

finding: The safest packet-152 lane-1 board split is the active outing locator family: resolveSeasonOutingLocator and its ActiveOutingLocator return shape.

The locator is already a pure exported seam shared by field-season-board.ts and field-request-state.ts, and existing tests cover Root Hollow, Season Threads, High Pass active, ready-to-file, filed, atlas-note, and copy-budget states.
Station subtitle is already outside field-season-board.ts in field-season-wrap.ts, while atlas/archive extraction would carry more adjacent copy-state decisions for this pass.
main_scope: Move ActiveOutingLocator and resolveSeasonOutingLocator into the new module.

Move only the minimal private expedition-progress helpers needed by the locator into the new module, or create one tiny shared helper module if avoiding duplicate expedition constants keeps the board cleaner.
Update field-season-board.ts and field-request-state.ts imports so the returned locator state and all downstream station, atlas, journal, route-marker, and world-map replay behavior remain identical.
non_goals: No changes to resolveFieldSeasonBoardState, resolveFieldAtlasState, resolveFieldSeasonExpeditionState, resolveFieldSeasonArchiveState, resolveFieldStationSubtitle, route definitions, save schema, rendering, authored copy, geometry, or journal layout.
No new season page, station card, route, expedition, world-map HUD, planner, migration, or copy rewrite.

result: Extracted ActiveOutingLocator, resolveSeasonOutingLocator, and the shared Root Hollow expedition progress helpers/constants into src/engine/field-season-outing-locator.ts.
Updated field-season-board.ts to consume the extracted helpers while preserving the existing resolveSeasonOutingLocator and ActiveOutingLocator re-export.
Updated field-request-state.ts to import the active outing locator directly from the new module.
scope_notes: No route-board state, atlas/archive state, expedition card state, station subtitle behavior, route definitions, save schema, rendering, authored copy, geometry, journal layout, or new UI surface changed.
verification: Passed: web-game client smoke against http://127.0.0.1:5174 with output/web-game/shot-0.png and output/web-game/state-0.json; no errors-0.json was produced

finding: The active outing locator extraction is behavior-preserving and keeps a compatibility re-export from field-season-board.ts.
field-request-state.ts now imports the locator directly from field-season-outing-locator.ts, reducing dependency on the large board resolver.
Root Hollow, Season Threads, High Pass active, High Pass ready-to-file, filed High Pass suppression, atlas notes, and copy-budget paths remain covered by existing tests.
Lane 1 already extracted the active outing locator family into src/engine/field-season-outing-locator.ts and reviewed the split as behavior-preserving.
Lane 4 already added route-state alignment coverage after the split.
Lane 3 should stay visual-proof-only for this packet unless station board placement screenshots expose a real regression.
main_scope: Capture current browser screenshots under ignored output/lane-3-main-416-board-placement-proof/.
Use window.get_debug_save_snapshots() to stage existing station board save states instead of hand-authoring saves.
Capture season-close-return, high-pass-ready-to-file, and high-pass-filed station route-board states with matching render_game_to_text JSON.
Add docs/reports/2026-04-20-field-season-board-placement-proof-implementation.md summarizing whether the split caused any visual placement delta.
non_goals: No changes to src/engine/field-season-board.ts, src/engine/field-season-outing-locator.ts, route definitions, save schema, station pages, authored copy, rendering, biome geometry, broad smoke coverage, route copy, or new screenshot framework unless visual proof exposes a real defect.

The season-close-return, high-pass-ready-to-file, and high-pass-filed station screenshots match their render_game_to_text states and show no new station board placement regression.

finding: The lane-1 extraction is complete and reviewed: field-season-board.ts keeps a compatibility re-export while field-request-state.ts imports resolveSeasonOutingLocator directly from field-season-outing-locator.ts.

Existing coverage protects the individual High Pass active, ready-to-file, filed, route-marker, and replay paths, but lane 4 should add one route-owned regression that compares request-state and board-state outputs together after the split.
The main pass should stay test-only unless the new regression exposes a real state mismatch.
main_scope: Extend src/test/field-requests.test.ts with a focused board-split route-state regression.
Assert active High Pass with selected route-marker still exposes active request, active outing, journal request, treeline marker, and Today: High Pass.
Assert ready-to-file High Pass keeps journal filing state while active outing, route marker, and replay label stay suppressed.
Assert filed High Pass suppresses active request, active outing, journal request, route marker, and replay label while the board remains filed.
Assert one replay window derives routeReplayLabel from routeBoard.replayNote for the focused target before falling back to active outing copy.
non_goals: No changes to field-season-board.ts, field-season-outing-locator.ts, route definitions, save schema, station pages, authored copy, geometry, rendering, framework code, or broad smoke coverage unless the focused regression finds a real defect.
No High Pass canonical filing identity, replay-title, support, or map-marker behavior changes.
